observations: Lines 1-19 open with a strong long/short braid: the 35.1% under-six-word sentence rate stays close to the two-fifths snap target, and the one-line beats ('The cold came up through the floor.' / 'Jaina's breathing.' / 'Alive.') earn emphasis through pressure rather than ornament.

Lines 51-59 vary the deposition cadence correctly: the longer recap sentence at line 51 is punctured by 'Slowed at Stratholme. Slowed again at Northrend.' so the clerk scene does not flatten into summary.

Lines 95-137 alternate clipped exchange and harder reflective sentences cleanly; 'It's looking.' / 'Real.' / 'The law was only one prison here.' shows the emphasis rule working without italics or explanation.

Lines 195-203 handle the exhibit reveal with proper horror rhythm: 'Cold rolled out first.' sets the hit, the middle sentence restores room geometry, and 'Not dead. Damaged.' closes with blunt tactical judgment.
Lines 520-573 keep the Lor'themar aftermath scene varied: the corridor report takes a fuller sentence, then the scene cuts back to 'Yes.' / 'Better.' / 'Go.' so Sylvanas still sounds pressure-tight.
Line 11 now reads 'a box had not solved,' which keeps the Frostmourne containment language concrete and contemptuous instead of abstract.
Line 173 originally drifted toward passive document-summary phrasing; 'Relay reports turned into preparation' now keeps Ashcombe's reframing active and in-scene instead of bureaucratic.
Lines 215-229 keep the palace mage in usable spoken language ('gets into people's heads,' 'rooms go cold,' 'rub their chests') rather than slipping into lab or memo diction.
Line 315 lets Antonidas sound formal without modern jargon: 'judge safely' and 'build a verdict around a weapon' fit an exhausted archmage speaking into court procedure.
Line 379 no longer says the sentence 'sounded administrative'; 'wanted to pass for procedure' keeps Sylvanas's judgment sharp and lived instead of meta-editorial.

fixes.before: Relay reports were reread as preparation.

fixes.rule: vocabularyStandards.avoid — cut process/document voice from narration
fixes.before: The sentence sounded administrative because that was what they wanted: living prisoners, not dead martyrs.
fixes.after: The sentence wanted to pass for procedure because that was what they wanted: living prisoners, not dead martyrs.

observations: The chapter's surviving image pool stays inside allowed domains: court procedure, bodily strain, weapon pressure, and cold under stone; I found no mixed-source or stacked metaphor chains.
Line 55's 'as if the page mattered more than whether the page was true' earns its place because it sharpens the clerk's priorities and stays in the courtroom domain.
Line 191's 'as if they were carrying a bier' is one of the few chapter-level set-piece images and clarifies status, dread, and room behavior in one stroke.
Line 219 keeps one spoken 'as if' in witness dialogue; it reads as ordinary testimony rather than ornamental simile and supports the court's fear response.

The chapter had seven 'as if' constructions on entry, breaking the anti-slop cap; I cut four weak scaffolds and left three load-bearing comparisons, taking the chapter from 7 to 3 without flattening the voice.

fixes.rule: metaphorPolicy.forbidden / antiSlop simile overload — weak comparison scaffold
fixes.before: The clerk glanced toward the masonry between the cells as if he might somehow see through it by looking hard enough.
fixes.after: The clerk glanced toward the masonry between the cells, trying to stare through it.

fixes.rule: perPOVCalibration + antiSlop simile overload — keep Jaina's visible strain external and concrete
fixes.before: She moved too straight, every step placed as if disorder itself might spill her open.
fixes.after: She moved too straight, every step one breath from spilling her open.

fixes.rule: metaphorPolicy.rule — replace decorative comparison with concrete bodily effect
fixes.before: Another kept swallowing before every breath, as if the air had turned rough inside his throat.
fixes.after: Another kept swallowing before every breath, throat working against air that scraped on the way in.

fixes.before: One clerk rubbed his breastbone as if trying to settle a sudden chill inside it.
fixes.after: One clerk rubbed his breastbone, trying to settle a sudden chill inside it.

observations: Average paragraph length sits at 17.67 words, and the longest paragraph stays under the 120-word / 5-6 sentence ceiling, so the chapter fits the 1-4 sentence cadence spec.
Lines 1-19 obey the scene-balance rule: after the denser room inventory at lines 3-13, the prose returns immediately to Jaina's breathing and then to the clerk's entrance.
The trial sequence avoids visual brickwork by alternating one-line hits ('The vault was there.' / 'Only the center was occupied.') with fuller procedural paragraphs.
Transitions mostly move by consequence, not clock time: statement -> blade clock -> tribunal frame -> exhibit -> verdict -> cell countdown -> Lor'themar arrival all carry pressure forward.

The two explicit time markers ('Hours later' at line 139 and the kitchen-smell bridge at line 441) are clarity anchors rather than lazy drift; I checked them against the transition spec and left them because the chapter would lose procedural legibility without them.

Quoted-word measurement lands around 14:86 dialogue-to-narration, below the global 45:55 target; I logged this as a scene-type discrepancy rather than a fix because imprisonment/tribunal/aftermath material naturally skews narration-heavy and the no-add-content rule forbids padding dialogue just to hit a number.
Attribution is clean: 24 no-frills said/asked/replied tags versus 1 creative tag, which keeps the attributed-line mix safely inside the style guide's plain-tag preference.
Lines 213-229 use the preferred action-hinge pattern: Ashcombe's question, the mage's answer, and Jaina's visible reaction all hand off through posture and room pressure instead of ornate tags.
The Sylvanas/Jaina wall exchanges at lines 91-129 and 397-437 work with sparse tagging and clean two-speaker clarity; no speaker-tracking failures surfaced.
Lor'themar's late scene keeps power in the spoken lines without over-tagging: posture, corridor access, and the wrist-grip do the attribution work the guide wants action beats to do.
Lines 3-13 stay fully Sylvanas-filtered: she maps paces, restraints, grate placement, torch quality, and guard behavior before she names emotion.
Lines 141-147 keep Jaina external-only; the revised stair beat now reads as visible bodily strain rather than narrated interior diagnosis, which matches the limited-third absolute rule.
Lines 171-178 show Sylvanas watching Thornton instead of Ashcombe once the legal machine is running, which fits her threat/authority-first thought pattern.
Lines 231-233 let Sylvanas read Jaina through locked hands, sweat, and the wish for a bowstring, not through translated sympathy monologue.
Lines 451-538 render Lor'themar through road dust, corridor leverage, bruise count, and field shorthand; the reunion sounds like Sylvanas reading a surviving ally, not an auditor narrating feelings.
Adverbs land at 46 total, or roughly 2.3 per 250-word page, which stays under the max 4 per page floor.
Passive voice measures at 3.74% by sentence-level heuristic, which stays just under the 4% ceiling; I checked the flagged passives and found no high-signal need for further cuts.
Filter words remain sparse and mostly load-bearing: line 85's 'She saw it land' and line 532's 'he saw her react' both mark tactical reads rather than generic sensory padding.
The chapter maintains the showing-to-telling floor: even the verdict paragraph at lines 377-381 uses telling only to compress legal procedure before the prose returns to chains, bodies, and cold.

Hard-limit checks passed after revision: as-if count is now 3, like count is 1, said/asked/replied dominates attribution, and no passive/adverb fix was needed to make the chapter specification-compliant.

Exposition integration passes: lines 11-13, 215-219, and 470-478 deliver Frostmourne mechanics through live custody pressure, witness testimony, and Lor'themar's arrival rather than detached narrator aside.

Power dynamics pass cleanly in the tribunal: Ashcombe controls interruption and frame with short declaratives, while Uther and Antonidas are forced into longer, more defensive clauses exactly as the guide prescribes.

Scene shape also passes: the chapter opens in-medias-res inside the cells, pivots hard when Frostmourne enters the courtroom, then resolves on uneasy operational pressure rather than emotional closure.
Speculative integration matches the domestication rule. Frostmourne is never treated as decorative wonder; every mention cashes out as cold, breach risk, ward failure, or targeting behavior.

Subtext density is strong in the Vereesa and Lor'themar scenes: the official topic is procedure and witnesses, while the actual exchange carries abandonment, family fracture, and late-arrival guilt under the surface.

Action pacing is used sparingly and correctly; fragments cluster only around the sword reveal, the verdict, and Jaina's counting beats, so the chapter does not fake action rhythm where the scene is really legal pressure.
Humor obeys the horror curve. The chapter permits only dry remnants ('Humans improve once condemned women become someone else's problem') and never lets wit undercut atrocity or prison pressure.
I re-read lines 188-237 after the simile cuts. The exhibit scene still sounds like Sylvanas observing a room under weapon-pressure, not like an auditor sanding the prose flat.
I re-read lines 372-381 after the line 379 rewrite. The verdict still lands as institutional violence first; the new wording sharpens POV and does not dilute the procedural ugliness.
I re-read lines 447-573 after the Lor'themar pass. The late-arrival scene keeps its exhausted dryness, and none of the compliance edits pushed it into sentimental explanation.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — lines 3-13, 77, 137, 379, and 451-538 stay lean, martial, and court-aware without modern jargon or generic fantasy filler.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — short declaratives dominate, but the prose widens at lines 171-178 and 468-482 when Sylvanas is stacking institutional consequence before snapping back to blunt judgment.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — low density overall; surviving images come from prison, court, weapon, road, and body-strain domains rather than ornamental lyricism.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — anger, abandonment, and relief arrive as sharpened observation, order, or withheld reaction, not direct naming.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — threat assessment -> capability judgment -> decision remains intact in the cell inventory, courtroom read, blade timing, and Lor'themar handoff.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — she notices cold, gait, chain weight, corridor control, sightlines, bruise count, and the physical spread of danger before social framing.
fingerprintChecks.humorType: pass — dry contempt survives in clipped flashes only; no quippy riffing or authorial wink entered the chapter.
fingerprintChecks.powerPosture: pass — when Sylvanas can seize ground she does it with short commands and questions; when boxed in, the syntax lengthens only enough to show what she is holding back.
fingerprintChecks.speculativeComfort: pass — Frostmourne is always translated into utility, breach risk, and targeting behavior rather than mystical awe.
fingerprintChecks.expositionFilter: pass — she never explains military logic to herself; she notices leverage, rank, guard posture, and whether magic changes the tactical board.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — Jaina's spoken lines ('It's looking.' / 'Days.' / 'Elven mages might feel it.' / 'Faster.') stay field-plain and pain-thinned, matching the chapter's external-only requirement.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — the chapter correctly strips her to short, exact lines under duress instead of giving her leisurely analytical clauses she cannot physically sustain here.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — Jaina's lane remains almost image-free in this chapter, which fits exhaustion and keeps the external read hard and clean.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — fear and overstrain show through locked hands, ragged breath, chain-tapping, and the failed laugh at line 435, not through overt self-labeling.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — even externally, she still moves from observation to model to warning: the blade is looking, the timetable is shorter, elven mages may corroborate it, the pace is getting faster.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — her scenes stay tied to cold, strain, ward pressure, and the bodily cost of hearing the blade through stone.
fingerprintChecks.humorType: pass — the one dry line ('That wasn't optimism.') lands as tired honesty rather than banter.
fingerprintChecks.powerPosture: pass — when physically outmatched she uses clipped analysis as shield; once reduced to the wall, the analysis still carries the operative fact.
fingerprintChecks.speculativeComfort: pass — she continues to treat the blade as structure, pressure, and timing rather than spectacle.
fingerprintChecks.expositionFilter: pass — even hurt, she offers only the tactically necessary translation another character can use.

metrics.dialogueToNarrationRatio: 14:86 by quoted-word count; logged as scene-type discrepancy, not fixed
